sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Close #3282
Modifications

Pin memory and do in-place updates when CUDA graph is enabled.
The cuda graph has been enabled for lora by default.

Benchmarking result
To do benchmarking for lora, run this command to launch server:
# Triton backend
python benchmark/lora/launch_server.py --ma

vllm.title: [Model] Add Granite Speech Support
vllm.author: alex-jw-brooks
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds support for Granite Speech models, and is a port of the corresponding PR in Transformers. The model uses a conformer-based encoder, with a blip2 qformer-based projector to encode the audio, and masks it into a granite LLM. This model also uses an audio-specific lora adapter, which shoul

Start a security guide in the project docs. The primary topic covered is
the importance of securing the network used for communications between
vLLM nodes.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: Improve conversion from dataclass configs to argparse arguments
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
optional_x has been replaced with optional_type(x) so we don't need different methods for different types
proper deprecation warning and timeline added for nullabke_kvs
tuple args can now have specific number of values if the config does not use Ellipsis
list args are also supported now
Optional con

vllm.title: Make name of `compressed-tensors` quant method consistent across vLLM
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The compressed tensors quantization method was referred to as compressed-tensors and compressed_tensors in different places, they've now been standardised to compressed-tensors.
Issue was discovered in #17130.
Splitting the fix into this PR to reduce the review load on the original.

vllm.title: Explicitly explain quant method override ordering and ensure all overrides are ordered
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Quantization method overrides are more clearly commented and enforced in config.py.
Issue was discovered in #17130.
Splitting the fix into this PR to reduce the review load on the original.

vllm.title: [Security] Don't bind tcp zmq socket to all interfaces
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This change addresses GHSA-9f8f-2vmf-885j
Stop binding to all interfaces for the XPUB socket in shm_broadcast.py.
We already determine the address that other hosts will use to connect to
this socket. If VLLM_HOST_IP is set, that will be the value. By binding
to all sockets, we expose private data to

vllm.title: [Chore] cleanup license indicators in light of SPDX
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR cleans up the license indicator within project description in light of the SPDX naming under project.license, which vLLM already include.
See https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/guides/writing-pyproject-toml/#license
Signed-off-by: Aaron Pham contact@aarnphm.xyz

Thanks to authors @JustinTong0323, @yhyang201, @whybeyoung, @GeLee-Q, and @yizhang2077 for originally producing these configs in

vllm.title: [Benchmark] Add single turn MTBench to Serving Bench
vllm.author: ekagra-ranjan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds single turn MTBench to benchmark datasets.
We have been using single turn MTBench for EAGLE bench using/offline_inference/eagle.py. However, it outputs output/s which does not ignore the TTFT. To measure TPOT we have to use benchmark_serving.py. We already have a lot of results on MTBen

vllm.title: [Optim] Compute multimodal hash only once per item
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Introduce ProcessingCache.get_item to simultaneously get the hash and the corresponding multi-modal item from the cache, so that we don't have to recompute the hash again in BaseMultiModalProcessor.apply.
Notes:

I intentionally omitted debug logging in ProcessingCache.get_item to avoid merge confli

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Remove hardcoding of model arch name in Eagle1/3 and use a config driven approac

vllm.author: luccafong
vllm.bodyExcerpt: make llama4 compatible with pure dense layers when interleave_moe_layer_step == 0 (no moe layers)

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix `numel()` downcast in fused_layernorm_dynamic_per_token_quant.cu
vllm.author: r-barnes
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary: numel() has type int64_t. The implicit downcasts fix in this change artificially truncate data ranges which can lead to hard-to-debug errors and SEVs. Using auto ensures that the correct data type is used.
Reviewed By: dtolnay
Differential Revision: D73608869

vllm.title: Ignore `'<string>'` filepath
vllm.author: zou3519
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
vLLM tries to get a list of files that Dynamo traced through. Some of these files can be equal to '<string>'. This may happen if the code object was dynamically generated, which does happen in some dataclasses cases.
This is one of the issues that was blocking the upgrade of vLLM from PyTorch 2.6 to

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Add contiguous call inside rope kernel wrapper
vllm.author: timzsu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes #16658.
Following @LucasWilkinson's suggestion, I followed the second way: add .contiguous() call in the kernel wrappers. Although making the query contiguous is sufficient for RoPE in the MLA backend, I found that a non-contiguous key is also likely to lead to the same issue because t

vllm.title: [Misc] Add a Jinja template to support Mistral3 function calling
vllm.author: chaunceyjiang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix #16292
Usage:
vllm serve mistralai/Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503 --chat-template examples/tool_chat_template_mistral3.jinja --tool-call-parser mistral --enable-auto-tool-choice

vllm.title: [Model] support MiniMax-VL-01 model
vllm.author: qscqesze
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
CLOSES #12073
This PR is intended to support the MiniMaxVL01 model inference.
It adopts the “ViT-MLP-LLM” framework, which is a commonly used technique in the field of multimodal large language models. Add the MiniMaxVL01 model inference implementation, and a image processer.

vllm.title: [Misc] Move config fields to MultiModalConfig
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR moves multi-modal related settings from ModelConfig to MultiModalConfig.
